content: Acme Corp's procurement team flagged SOC2 Type II as blockers for contract signature. Their CISO won't greenlight $180k deal without evidence. No timeline on when they'd revisit.
summary: Acme Corp blocks $180k deal on missing SOC2

content: Pattern emerging: enterprise buyers (>500 headcount) now expect SOC2 Type II before pilots. This wasn't friction 6mo ago. Competitors likely got certified; we're now behind baseline expectation.
summary: SOC2 Type II now table-stakes for enterprise deals

content: Lina reported Initech's champion wants to move forward but procurement blocked her. She estimated $220k ACV, 12-month contract. Waiting for SOC2 or they walk to competitor.
summary: Initech champion supportive; procurement veto on compliance gap

content: To unblock deals, initiate SOC2 Type II audit: (1) engage external auditor (budget ~$40-60k), (2) implement control documentation across AWS/Snowflake/DataDog, (3) 3-6mo timeline for report delivery.
summary: SOC2 Type II audit process: auditor, controls, 6mo runway

content: Sebastien asked for deal impact quantification. Identified 5 stalled opportunities: Acme ($180k), Initech ($220k), Globex ($185k), Wayne Industries ($95k), unnamed prospect ($70k). Total ~$750k ARR at risk.
summary: $750k ARR blocked across 5 deals waiting on SOC2

content: Anya called: finance needs SOC2 spend in Q2 budget line. I advised $55k for auditor + ~$20k internal controls implementation + Marcus's team time. She flagged CFO will push back on cost.
summary: Anya flagged budget pressure for SOC2 audit costs

content: SOC2 Type II requires 6-month control observation period, meaning earliest report delivery is mid-September 2026. We won't unblock these deals before then unless we find expedited auditor.
summary: SOC2 timeline bottleneck: 6mo observation window minimum

content: I've been stuck re-explaining SOC2 context to Sebastien 3x. Need to send him a written pattern memo so I stop repeating myself and he internalizes the deal risk without meetings.
summary: Recognizing need for async memo to avoid re-explaining SOC2

content: Drafted memo to Sebastien: 'No SOC2 evidence, no deployment.' Listed 5 deals, timeline constraints, budget ask. He replied same day: 'Approved. Get auditor quotes by end of week.'
summary: Sebastien greenlit SOC2 pursuit after memo

content: When screening SOC2 auditors, validate: (1) SaaS audit experience, (2) AWS/Snowflake/Anthropic compliance knowledge, (3) 3-4mo audit timeline feasibility, (4) fixed fee structure.
summary: Auditor selection criteria: SaaS, vendor knowledge, timeline, pricing

content: Received 3 auditor proposals. Deloitte quoted $65k (5mo timeline), CliftonLarsonAllen $48k (4mo), and boutique firm $42k (vague on controls). Recommended CLA to Sebastien.
summary: Three auditor quotes; recommended CLA for balance of cost/timeline

content: SOC2 audit will require Ari and Devon to document control design for SQL/data access, encryption at rest/transit, access logging in DataDog/Sentry. Engineering time non-negotiable for credible report.
summary: Engineering deep involvement required for SOC2 evidence gathering

content: Ari pushed back: 'Controls doc will take 40+ eng hours.' Told him to block it on roadmap now or auditor will delay us. He reluctantly added it to Q2 planning. Tom flagged concern too but deferred to Ari.
summary: Ari reluctantly committed 40h eng time to controls documentation

content: Marcus reached out: CS needs to document incident response procedures for SOC2 auditors. Current playbooks are ad-hoc. I told him to formalize and get approval by end of April or audit timeline slips.
summary: CS incident response procedures must be formalized for audit

content: Globex's procurement team asked for timeline on SOC2 report delivery. I said mid-September 2026 best case. They countered: can't wait past June for pilot eval. Deal likely dead unless we accelerate.
summary: Globex timeline incompatible with SOC2 audit window

content: Identified pattern: buyers under 6-month procurement windows will slip away regardless of SOC2. Future lens: enterprise deals need 9-month runway assumption or expect deal decay.
summary: Enterprise deal timeline mismatch with compliance cycles

content: Wayne Industries champion emailed: their audit board changed requirements mid-contract, now demanding SOC2 + HIPAA evidence. I escalated to Sebastien; scope creep risk for $95k deal.
summary: Wayne Industries added HIPAA requirement mid-negotiation

content: Moving forward, flag SOC2 gaps in opportunity qualification: ask 'do security reqs exist?' and 'timeline on approval?' at first call. Route to me if yes. Don't waste Lina/Jenny time on deals stuck on compliance.
summary: Qualify SOC2/compliance blockers early in deal cycles
